readme: Evodocs are repo-local project understanding documents for coding agents. Start with .evodocs/modules/module-index.json to identify the relevant project modules, then read the matching .evodocs/modules/mod--<module>.md files, including parent, child, or neighboring module docs when a change crosses boundaries. Use those docs to understand responsibilities, flows, relationships, constraints, and known limits; inspect the actual code when more detail or verification is needed. Only after collecting enough context from the index, module docs, and necessary code should you analyze the project, design a plan, or modify code.

governance.single_writer: n-evodocs helper (scripts/evodocs.py); no other tool writes .evodocs/
governance.cadence: rewritten whenever an evodocs capability runs (index create/recreate, module-doc apply, commit-diff update)
governance.authoritative_intent: .prodfarm/charter/ is the project intent (human/prodfarm); evodocs never authors intent, it only reverses documentation from code
